files_changed.change: Add a guard clause in DecodeTypedRecord rejecting RecordTypeInvalid (0) and any out-of-range/unrecognized RecordType byte with an explicit fmt.Errorf, before constructing/returning the TypedRecord.

Add TestDecodeTypedRecordRejectsInvalidType covering Type=0 and Type=out-of-range (e.g. 6, 255); assert both return non-nil errors. Extend/confirm existing round-trip test still passes for all 5 valid constants (no regression).

callers_of_DecodeTypedRecord: engine/wal/recovery.go (replay path, presumed reader of ReadSegment output)
risk: Low. New validation only tightens an existing decode path; all currently-valid on-disk RecordType byte values (1-5) remain accepted unchanged. No changes to encode path, no changes to on-disk format. Downstream callers that only ever decode well-formed records written by this same package's Encode functions are unaffected; callers reading corrupted/foreign data now get an explicit error instead of silently misinterpreting bytes.

prior_landed_work.subtask_4.5.4.2: commit 5937be9 removed orphaned AppendAndApply doc-comment block from engine/wal/record.go; verified PASS. This run builds on top of that commit's record.go state, not in conflict (different region of file: doc-comment block was ~lines 342-370, this change is in DecodeTypedRecord ~line 103-113).
